events.event_description: George Wadsworth appointed as 'Agent and Consul General' to provide a quasi-diplomatic presence in Beirut.

evidence.quote: The United States appointed George Wadsworth as "Agent and Consul General" on October 9, 1942, to provide a quasi-diplomatic presence in Beirut until the United States determined that Lebanon achieved effective independence in 1944.

events.event_description: The United States established diplomatic relations and the American Legation in Lebanon when George Wadsworth presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: The United States established diplomatic relations with Lebanon when George Wadsworth presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary on November 16, 1944.

events.event_description: The American Legation in Beirut was raised to embassy status when Harold B. Minor was appointed Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in Beirut was raised to embassy status on October 3, 1952, when Harold B. Minor was appointed Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

events.event_description: The United States withdrew all embassy personnel from the American Embassy in Beirut, leaving no diplomatic representation in Lebanon.

evidence.quote: The United States withdrew all personnel including Ambassador John Thomas McCarthy from the American Embassy in Beirut on September 6, 1989.

events.event_description: The American Embassy in Beirut was reopened when new Ambassador Ryan Crocker presented his credentials.

evidence.quote: The American Embassy in Beirut was reopened on November 29, 1990, when new Ambassador Ryan Crocker presented his credentials.
